# Session 7: Field ID Mapping - Toggle Fix & Page Numbering Discovery

**Date:** December 17, 2025
**Project:** APR Dashboard - Preview Master Report
**Session Name:** Field ID Mapping
**Agent:** Claude Code

---

## 🔍 Search Keywords

`data-sample`, `toggle-fix`, `field-mapping`, `page-numbering`, `visual-pages`, `footer-pages`, `HTML-page-vs-visual`, `property-overview`, `highest-best-use`, `introduction-executive-summary`, `PREVIEW-Master.html`, `TOGGLE-TEST.html`

---

## 📋 Session Overview

This session resolved a critical issue where toggle functionality wasn't working on early pages, discovered a page numbering mismatch between HTML attributes and visual footer numbers, and successfully mapped 51 fields across Pages 1-3.

---

## 🔧 Major Discoveries

### 1. Page Numbering Mismatch
- **Problem:** User reported toggle not working on "Page 3" and "Page 4"
- **Discovery:** HTML uses `data-page-num="Page X"` attributes, but user sees different page numbers in the footer
- **Root Cause:** Visual page numbers in footer (`<span class="page-num">3</span>`) are the SOURCE OF TRUTH, not HTML page attributes
- **Example Mapping:**
  - Visual Page 3 (footer shows "3") = HTML `data-page-num="Page 8"`
  - Visual Page 1 (footer shows "1") = HTML `data-page-num="Page 6"`

### 2. Initial Work Was on Wrong Pages
- First attempted edits were on HTML "Page 3" and "Page 4" (Letter of Transmittal and Conditions pages)
- These pages don't show page numbers in their footers - they're unnumbered intro pages
- User was looking at different pages entirely (the numbered report pages)
- Solution: Search for pages with `<span class="page-num">` to find actual numbered pages

### 3. Toggle Mechanism Works Perfectly
- Created standalone test page (TOGGLE-TEST.html) with identical format
- Test page toggle works 100% correctly
- Proved format was correct; issue was page identification, not implementation

---

## ✅ Solutions Implemented

### Format Verification
```html
<span class="field-mapped" data-sample="VALUE" title="{{Field_ID}}">{{Field_ID}}</span>
```
✅ **Confirmed correct** - Matches working pages (Page 40+) exactly

### Page Identification Protocol
1. Search HTML for `<span class="page-num">N</span>` in footer
2. Trace back to parent `data-page-num="Page X"` attribute
3. Extract field-mapped spans between that marker and footer
4. Map field IDs to PNG image values

### Data Extraction Workflow
1. Locate PNG image for visual page number
2. Read PNG to extract all property-specific values
3. Match PNG values to HTML field IDs
4. Add data-sample attributes with extracted values
5. Commit to git with clear message

## 📝 Key Instructions for Next Agent

If continuing this work:

1. **Use visual page numbers** (footer) as reference, NOT HTML data-page-num
2. **Search for pages** using: `grep -n '<span class="page-num">N</span>'`
3. **Extract data from PNG images:** `/doc-page-images/Page-NN.png`
4. **Format data-sample** exactly as shown: `data-sample="VALUE"`
5. **Commit after each page** with clear git message
6. **Update FIELD-DATA-REFERENCE.md** with each new page

## 💡 Technical Notes

### Toggle JavaScript Logic
```javascript
// When toggle ON: Replace field text with data-sample value
const sampleValue = field.getAttribute('data-sample');
if (sampleValue) {
    field.textContent = sampleValue;
}

// When toggle OFF: Restore original field ID text
const originalText = field.getAttribute('data-original');
if (originalText) {
    field.textContent = originalText;
}
```

### Why Previous Work Failed
- Early attempts added data-sample to HTML "Page 3" (Letter) and "Page 4" (Conditions)
- These pages are UNNUMBERED (no footer page number)
- User was viewing NUMBERED pages with different HTML page numbers
- Solution: Match visual page numbers with HTML locations via footer search

### Working Example (Page 40+)
Page 40 in HTML already has correct format:
```html
<span class="field-mapped" data-sample="650" title="{{Market_Rent_1Bed_Comp1_UnitSize}}">{{Market_Rent_1Bed_Comp1_UnitSize}}</span>
```
This page's toggle WORKS because data-sample is present. Same format applied to Pages 1-3.
